slides.title: Processes & Workflows
slides.subtitle: Design, automate, and manage business processes end to end

slides.title: What is a Kissflow Process?
bullets: A process combines a Form + Workflow + Reports into one solution
Automates multi-step business operations with defined participants
Each submission creates a process item that flows through steps
Supports sequential, parallel, and conditional branching
Available on Basic and Enterprise plans
Can be standalone or embedded inside Kissflow Apps
slides.notes: Explain the three pillars: form, workflow, reports.

slides.title: Process Builder Interface
bullets: Left panel: Form, Workflow, Reports, Settings, and Permissions tabs
Center canvas: visual workflow designer with drag-and-drop steps
Right panel: step configuration — participants, actions, conditions
Form designer integrated — build data capture alongside workflow
Preview mode to test the process before publishing
Version history and draft/publish workflow for safe changes
slides.notes: Demo the process builder layout live.

slides.title: Workflow Step Types
bullets: Start step — entry point when initiator submits the form
Approval step — participant approves, rejects, or sends back
Input step — collect additional data from assigned user
Parallel branch — multiple steps execute simultaneously
Condition step — route based on form field values or rules
End step — mark item complete and trigger post-completion actions
slides.notes: Draw a simple 3-step approval on the whiteboard or screen.

slides.title: Assigning Participants
bullets: Assign by specific user, group, role, or form field value
Dynamic assignment — manager of initiator, department head, etc.
Multiple participants per step with all-must-approve or any-one rules
Substitute/delegate configuration for absence handling
External participants via email for steps outside the organization
Watcher support — inform stakeholders without workflow involvement
slides.notes: Cover dynamic assignment — very commonly used pattern.

slides.title: Process Item Lifecycle
bullets: Initiator fills form and clicks Submit — item enters workflow
Item moves step-by-step based on workflow design and conditions
Each participant receives notification and sees item in My Tasks
Actions: Approve, Reject, Send Back, Save Draft, Delegate
Status tracked: In Progress, Completed, Rejected, Withdrawn
Full activity log with timestamps, comments, and attachments
slides.notes: Demo submitting a leave request or purchase order process.

slides.title: Notifications and SLAs
bullets: Email notifications on assignment, approval, rejection, and completion
In-app notification bell and My Tasks integration
Mobile push notifications via Kissflow mobile app
SLA timers — define expected completion time per step
Escalation rules — notify manager when SLA is breached
Reminder emails for pending items approaching deadline
slides.notes: Show notification settings in process configuration.

slides.title: Process Permissions
bullets: Process Admin — full edit access to form, workflow, and settings
Initiator — can start new items and view own submissions
Participant — can act on assigned steps only
Report Viewer — access reports without process participation
Field-level permissions per workflow step (show/hide/edit)
Item-level access — initiator sees all, others see assigned steps
slides.notes: Permissions are step-aware — same field can be read-only at one step, editable at another.

slides.title: Process Watchers
bullets: Watchers stay informed without participating in the workflow
Add watchers when creating an item or from item detail view
Watchers receive notifications on status changes and comments
No approval rights — visibility and awareness only
Useful for managers, auditors, compliance, and cross-functional teams
Also available when processes are embedded inside Kissflow Apps

slides.title: Lookup Columns in Process Tables
bullets: Lookup fields bring data from other flows and datasets into forms
New feature: lookup column values visible directly in table rows
See multiple lookup details at a glance without opening each row
Reduces clicks when working with line-item tables (PO lines, expenses)
Configure lookup display columns in form designer
Announced June 2026 — Product announcement
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com — Introducing lookup columns in tables

slides.title: Process Reports — Overview
bullets: Every process can have multiple reports for data analysis
Reports pull data from completed and in-progress process items
Access reports from process menu or embed in apps
Report permissions separate from process participation
Export to CSV, Excel, and PDF for external sharing
Schedule automated report delivery via email
slides.notes: KEY SLIDE — Reports are a core part of every process solution.

slides.title: Creating Process Reports
bullets: Open process builder → Reports tab → Create new report
Select fields from form, system fields, and workflow metadata
Apply filters: status, date range, participant, department, etc.
Choose visualization: table, bar chart, line chart, pie chart, pivot
Group and aggregate data — count, sum, average, min, max
AI-assisted report creation — describe report in natural language
slides.notes: Demo creating a 'Pending Approvals by Department' report.

slides.title: Process Report Types and Use Cases
bullets: Status reports — items by stage, overdue, completed this week
Performance reports — average approval time per step/participant
Volume reports — submissions per month, department, category
Compliance reports — rejected items, send-backs, SLA breaches
Pivot reports — cross-tabulate dimensions for executive dashboards
Embed reports in app pages for role-based operational dashboards
slides.notes: Give examples relevant to the audience's industry.

slides.title: Report Scheduling and Distribution
bullets: Schedule reports to run daily, weekly, or monthly
Email report as attachment to defined recipient list
Combine multiple reports into dashboard views within apps
Report viewers do not need process initiator/participant access
Export large datasets for BI tools (Power BI, Tableau)
API access to report data for custom integrations
slides.notes: Show schedule configuration in report settings.

slides.title: Processes Inside Apps
bullets: Embed processes as components on app pages
Initiate and track process items without leaving the app context
Combine process data with dataforms, boards, and reports on one page
App-level navigation ties multiple processes into one solution
Process watchers work inside app-embedded processes
Role-based page visibility controls who sees which processes

slides.title: Process Best Practices
bullets: Start simple — 3-5 steps, expand as requirements clarify
Use clear step names and instructions for participants
Test with a small pilot group before organization-wide rollout
Define SLAs early — they drive accountability and reporting
Create at least one operational report before going live
Document the process purpose and escalation path for users
slides.notes: Close Processes section — transition to Apps or Forms next.
